2605.06623.title: MASPO: Joint Prompt Optimization for LLM-based Multi-Agent Systems
2605.06623.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based Multi-agent systems (MAS) have shown promise in tackling complex collaborative tasks, where agents are typically orchestrated via role-specific prompts. While the quality of these prompts is pivotal, jointly optimizing them across interacting agents remains a non-trivial challenge, primarily due to the misalignment between local agent objectives and holistic system goals. To address this, we introduce MASPO, a novel framework designed to automatically and iteratively refine prompts across the entire system. A core innovation of MASPO is its joint evaluation mechanism, which assesses prompts not merely by their local validity, but by their capacity to facilitate downstream success for successor agents. This effectively bridges the gap between local interactions and global outcomes without relying on ground-truth labels. Furthermore, MASPO employs a data-driven evolutionary beam search to efficiently navigate the high-dimensional prompt space. Extensive empirical evaluations across 6 diverse tasks demonstrate that MASPO consistently outperforms state-of-the-art prompt optimization methods, achieving an average accuracy improvement of 2.9. We release our code at https://github.com/wangzx1219/MASPO.
2605.06623.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）為基礎的多代理系統（MAS）在處理複雜的協作任務方面顯示出潛力，這些任務中代理通常通過角色特定的提示進行協調。雖然這些提示的質量至關重要，但在互動代理之間共同優化它們仍然是一個非平凡的挑戰，主要是因為局部代理目標與整體系統目標之間的不一致。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了MASPO，一個旨在自動和迭代地在整個系統中精煉提示的新框架。MASPO的一個核心創新是其聯合評估機制，它不僅根據提示的局部有效性來評估它們，還根據它們促進後繼代理成功的能力來進行評估。這有效地彌合了局部互動與全球結果之間的差距，而不依賴於真實標籤。此外，MASPO利用數據驅動的進化束搜索來高效地導航高維提示空間。在6個不同任務上的廣泛實證評估表明，MASPO始終超越最先進的提示優化方法，實現了平均準確率提高2.9。我們的代碼已在 https://github.com/wangzx1219/MASPO 上發布。
2605.06623.author: Zhexuan Wang et.al.
2605.06623.authors: Zhexuan Wang, Xuebo Liu, Li Wang, Zifei Shan, Yutong Wang, Zhenxi Song, Min Zhang
